# FRAMEWORK PDF CAPTIONS — hti-hurricanes 2024-08-23
# framework_doc: https://www.unocha.org/attachments/eef584b2-141a-480e-aa32-a98ecc61825a/Haiti%20Cadre%20Action%20Anticipatoire%20Version%20Finale%2023.08.2024.pdf
# pages: 22 · model: sonnet · via: claude headless
# Auto-generated descriptions of the PDF's visuals (maps/charts/tables). PUBLIC.
------------------------------------------------------------
--- page 1 ---
Cover page and table of contents. Title: "Cadre d'Action Anticipatoire — Pilote en Haïti | Tempêtes/Ouragans", Version #1, 1 Août 2024, OCHA Haiti. Approved by Coordinatrice Résidente/Humanitaire 18 août 2024 and l'ERC 23 août 2024. Table of contents lists 13 sections: résumé exécutif, considérations principales, contexte du pays, cadre conceptuel, lien avec gestion des risques de catastrophes, modèle de déclenchement, protocole de suivi, livraison/plan d'action, financement préétabli, apprentissage, leçons d'autres pilotes, défis/améliorations, annexes.

--- page 2 ---
Executive summary. Haiti ranked 1st in global climate risk index; ~96% of Haitian population exposed to a range of climate risks. 2024 Atlantic season forecast: 85% probability of exceeding normal activity. Framework objective: AA pilot for cyclones/hurricanes covering mobilisation, anticipatory action, and early response phases.
Key trigger thresholds table (Tableau 1):
- Prévision / Mobilisation: return period 3.4 ans, lead time max 120h (5 jours), precipitation 42mm (2-day avg over country), wind speed 64 noeuds (Ouragan Catégorie 1), distance 230km
- Prévision / Action Anticipatoire: return period 4.8 ans, lead time 72h (3 jours), precipitation 42mm, wind 64 noeuds (Ouragan Catégorie 1), distance 230km
- Observation / Réponse Précoce: return period 4.0 ans, lead time n/a, precipitation 70mm, wind 50 noeuds (Tempête Tropicale), distance 230km
- Période de retour global: 3.0 ans
Data sources: NOAA NHC, CHIRPS-GEFS, NASA IMERG.

--- page 3 ---
Budget and geographic action plan. Tableau 2 — Répartition indicative du budget CERF:
- PAM: CERF $2,000,000 / Co-financing $2,000,000 / 8 implementing partners
- UNICEF: CERF $1,200,000 / Co-financing $500,000 / 7 partners
- OIM: CERF $800,000 / Co-financing —
- Total: CERF $4,000,000 / Co-financing $2,500,000
Geographic action plan (Tableau 3) — key rows:
- PAM / Sécurité alimentaire: Messages d'alerte précoce (multiples); cash transfers (multiples) → 75,000 persons → Grand Anse, Sud, Nord, Nord-Est, Ouest, Centre, Nord
- PAM total: 4,900,000 persons targeted → Artibonite, Ouest
- UNICEF / Eau, Hygiène: Distribution kits EAH; cash transfers (EAH) → 30,500 persons → Sud-Ouest, Sud-Est, Nippes, Artibonite, Ouest
- UNICEF Multisector: cash transfers multiples and sectorielles → 20,500 persons → Sud-Ouest, Sud-Est, Nippes, Artibonite, Ouest
- Partenaires UNICEF/AVSI: cash transfers multiples → Grand Sud
- Partenaires UNICEF (JDECM/JOG): cash transfers multiples → Ouest, Artibonite

--- page 4 ---
Geographic action plan continued. Additional partner rows:
- UNICEF Plan International / Eau, Hygiène: distribution kits EAH + dignity kits → Sud-est
- UNICEF Caritas / Eau, Hygiène: distribution kits EAH + dignity kits → Artibonite
- UNICEF ORRAH / Eau, Hygiène: distribution kits EAH + dignity kits → Ouest
- DIM / Abris, bien non alimentaires (ABNA): Services d'évacuation et gestion abris d'urgence; distribution de matériel → 96 abris d'urgence (19,200 persons) → Sud, Nord, Nord-Est, Ouest, Nippes, Centre
- DIM also: support aux brigadiers (1,000 persons) → Sud, Nord, Nord-Est, Ouest, Nippes, Centre
- DIM: monetary transfers sectorielles (abris, ABNA alimentaires) → 15,000 → Sud, Nord, Nord-Est, Ouest, Nippes, Centre
Geographic prioritization based on capacity of organizations and exposure of populations to cyclones/floods.

--- page 5 ---
Context: Tempêtes/Cyclones/Ouragans en Haïti. Haiti's geography in Caribbean makes it highly vulnerable. Between 1954–2022: 36 tropical systems classified; 15% touched 10+ departments causing major disruption.
Bar chart — "Percentage of tropical systems that hit Haiti between 1954 and 2022 by category":
- X-axis: storm category (TD, TS, Cat 1, Cat 2, Cat 3, Cat 4, Cat 5)
- Y-axis: percentage of occurrences (~0–40%)
- TD dominates at ~40%; TS ~20%; Cat 3 ~20%; Cat 1 ~15%; Cat 2 ~10%; Cat 4 ~5%; Cat 5 negligible
Impact by category:
- Category 2 or less (moderate): average 109,621 persons affected/storm; max 1,087,000 (Hurricane Gordon 1994)
- Category 3 or above: average 377,895 persons affected/storm; min 1,110; max 2,100,439

--- page 6 ---
Context continued: impact from category 4+. Average 440,224 persons touched per cat 4+ storm. UN coordinates with Haitian government (DGPC, MAST, Ministère de l'Agriculture) on disaster response. Coordination with SIMAST (labour/social affairs ministry) for beneficiary data. Section: Contexte conceptuel de l'action anticipatoire — defines anticipatory action concept; references international humanitarian coordination framework.

--- page 7 ---
Conceptual framework of anticipatory action. Figure 3: "Réduction de l'impact — d'un choc prévisible à travers l'action anticipatoire". Diagram shows left-to-right sequence: Alerte précoce → Préparation → Action Anticipatoire → Réponse Traditionnelle, with a declining impact curve illustrating that earlier action reduces humanitarian impact. Right side: "une réponse humanitaire plus vitale, plus efficace et plus digne." Four foundational elements of this framework listed below the diagram (multisector approach, cash transfers, development co-benefits, capturing learning).

--- page 8 ---
Principles and existing AA actions in Haiti. Principles: multisector approach; cash transfers as preferred modality (where markets/context permit); co-benefits of development; capturing learning; integration with long-term resilience. Existing AA actions:
- PAM: early warning plan (EWAA), national plan for emergency response (PNRU)
- UNICEF: operational strategy since fin 2023 with 2 implementing partners and 3 financial partners; CERF mechanism used for triggers
- National Adaptation Plan (PAN) for climate change references also noted

--- page 9 ---
Trigger model: forecasts and mechanism. Data sources for triggers:
- Wind speed maximum (noeuds): NHC (National Hurricane Center)
- Total precipitation 2-day average over all Haiti: CHIRPS-GEFS from CHC Santa Barbara; observations from IMERG
Trigger indicators table (Tableau 4 — Aperçu des déclencheurs):
- Prévision/Mobilisation: return 3.4 ans, max 120h lead, 42mm precip, 64 noeuds (Ouragan Cat 1), 230km
- Prévision/Action Anticipatoire: return 4.8 ans, 72h lead, 42mm precip, 64 noeuds (Ouragan Cat 1), 230km
- Observation/Réponse Précoce: return 4.0 ans, n/a, 70mm precip, 50 noeuds (Tempête Tropicale), 230km
- Période de retour global: 3.0 ans
Footnote: observational precipitation threshold slightly higher than forecast threshold due to systematic bias difference between CHIRPS and IMERG.

--- page 10 ---
Trigger timing and activation chronology. Tableau 5 — Moments de déclenchement:
- 1er: Based on forecasts → Mobilisation trigger: 2–5 days before landfall; max 5% of CERF budget
- 2ème: Based on forecasts → Anticipatory action trigger: 2–3 days before landfall; max 95% of CERF budget
- 3ème: Based on observational data → Early response actions: 1 day after landfall; max 95% of CERF budget
Figure 6: Chronologie timeline diagram — horizontal axis spans ~8 days total; shows:
- Day -5: mobilisation trigger possible (48h window)
- Day -3: anticipatory action trigger (72h window)
- Day 0: landfall (Passage du cyclone)
- Day +1 to +3: observational trigger / early response possible
Footnote: activation is possible at a given time if the system is expected within 5 days and precipitation conditions are met.

--- page 11 ---
Monitoring protocol. Tableau 6 — Protocole de suivi:
- Centre de Données Humanitaires (CHD): reads NOAA NHC, IMERG, CHIRPS-GEFS; sends info to OCHA Haiti, UNICEF, OM at each forecast update (every 6h from NHC)
- CHD: sends activation info to OCHA Haiti
- OCHA Haiti: sends activation messages to agencies; convenes Humanitarian Resident/Minister
- CERF: sends official approvals to agencies
- OCHA Haiti: launches activation; sends message to agencies
Section 8: Livraison — Plan d'action, ciblage, prioritisation géographique. Criteria for selecting anticipatory vs early-response actions listed.

--- page 12 ---
Selection criteria and preparation conditions. Three selection criteria for AA vs early response actions:
1. Capacity to deliver in the window of opportunity — can the action be effectively done before the cyclone?
2. Operational capacity — do agencies/partners have institutional, financial, administrative, logistical capacity?
3. No-regrets approach — do proposed actions benefit affected populations even if no disaster occurs?
Preparation conditions by agency:
- UNICEF: RapidPro system operational; beneficiary lists from SIMAST/SIGEE integrated; tested with 2 partners in 2023
- OIM: SCOPE beneficiary lists available; evaluation of targeted zones completed; DGPC brigadier lists finalized
- PAM: multiple preparatory measures covering all operations for alerting, mobilisation, response

--- page 13 ---
Geographic targeting and ciblage. Map: Haiti showing geographic prioritization for AA deployment.
- Map shows all departments of Haiti with agency logos (UNICEF, OIM, PAM) placed at their deployment locations
- North-West department excluded due to limited operational capacity
- Key targeted areas: Nord, Nord-Est, Ouest (incl. Port-au-Prince area), Sud, Sud-Est, Artibonite, Centre, Nippes
- Map uses pink/red shading indicating cyclone exposure risk levels
- Ciblage criteria: agencies use SIMAST or SIGEE (labour/social affairs) registries for vulnerability; PAM uses food security vulnerability criteria; UNICEF uses child vulnerability data from national education system (SIGEE) and displacement from IDP systems (HOPE); OIM and DGPC joint targeting for displaced populations

--- page 14 ---
Financing — CERF. Central Emergency Response Fund (CERF) eligibility conditions:
- Approved by Humanitarian Resident/Coordinator with candidate dossier + specific budget
- Approved by Coordinator/CERF emergency dossier (prior pre-agreed activation required)
- Pre-agreed trigger activation
Two approval steps required for each activation; each country can access up to $4M USD maximum per tropical storm.
Co-financing details:
- PAM: $2M USD allocated for emergency cash assistance to 15,000 households + preparation/activation costs; 15,000 ménages total
- UNICEF: operational strategy launched 2023 with 2 implementing partners + 3 financial partners

--- page 15 ---
Other funding sources. Other potential donors: bilateral donors, multi-lateral reserve funds. Section 10: Apprentissage (Learning). Post-distribution monitoring (PDM) by implementing agencies coordinated with CHD; evaluation committee convened by OCHA. CERF funds learning activities. S&E (Suivi et évaluation) key questions:
- Did the action have impact on survival or quality of life of beneficiaries?
- Was the timing right (did the activation happen fast enough)?
- What is the counterfactual (comparison with similar past disasters without AA)?
- Were there multiplier effects from different triggers?
Potentielle évaluation indépendante: from 2025 season and on, independent final evaluation planned to assess framework setup and activation.

--- page 16 ---
Lessons from other AA pilots and improvements proposed. Key challenges identified:
- Complexity of cyclone monitoring systems (trajectory, intensity both vary)
- Insufficient lead time in some scenarios
- Divergence between forecast and observational data
- Internal agency capacity requires training and specific SOPs for AA
Improvements: specific training on AA actions; SOPs and procedures developed for alert précoce and activation; joint testing undertaken in 2023.
Section 11: Financing and partners detail. Section 12: Défis et améliorations; Section 13: Annexes introduced.

--- page 17 ---
Annex: List of expert group members (Tableau 8). Table with columns: NO, TYPE D'ORGANISATION, ORGANISATION, NOM/PRÉNOM, POSITION. Key entries:
1. Agence de l'ONU / OCHA Haiti — Abdoulaye Sawadogo — Chef de Bureau
2. Agence de l'ONU / OCHA Haiti — Emmanuelle Pierre — Cheffe de l'Unité Coordination
3. ONG Internationale — Sheldra Italis — Chargée des Affaires Humanitaires (Point focal Action Anticipatoire)
4. ONG Internationale — Sebastien Gabriner — Point focal Gestion de l'Information
5. ONG Internationale / Plan International — Diego Fuentes — Emergency Response Manager (ERM)
6. ONG Internationale / Plan International — ? — DIM Advisor
7. ONG Internationale / Humanité et Inclusion — Jude Caere Pierre — Spécialiste en Gestion Inclusive des Risques
8. Gouvernement / DGPC — Mackenhey — Responsable de la Protection
9. ONG Internationale / FAO — Fabien Tallec — Emergency Coordinator
10. Agence de l'ONU / FAO — Claudio Costarino — Emergency Specialist

--- page 18 ---
Annex: Expert group members continued (rows 15–29). Includes representatives from: UNICEF, OIM, CRS, ONG nationale ACCODEF/PON, Katastrofinenhife (Diakonie), FONDATION DE RECHERCHE, UNFNR (Thierry MESSINA — Early Warning à All Haiti), CIMA Foundation (Enrico Ponte — Expert Réduction des Risques de Catastrophes). Example message section: shows "centre for humdata" button template for trigger notifications.
Message template: "Haïti : suivi des ouragans" / TEST_STORM_NAME — prévision 11h00 1 juil. 2024 (heure locale Haïti) / Déclencheur de mobilisation: ACTIF / Déclencheur d'action: ACTIF.

--- page 19 ---
Example activation message with visuals. Two figures:
1. NHC forecast track map: Caribbean/Atlantic basin showing TEST_STORM_NAME projected track passing through/near Haiti; track shown with cone of uncertainty; multiple historical storm tracks overlaid.
2. Scatter plot — "Comparaison de précipitations, vent, et impact — Seuil de distance = 230 km":
   - X-axis: Vitesse du vent maximum (noeuds), range 0–180
   - Y-axis: Précipitations produites (mm, 2-jour avg over country)
   - Historical storms shown as bubbles (bubble size = population affected)
   - Upper-right red zone: "Zone de déclenchement — Allocations CERF en rouge"
   - TEST_STORM_NAME plotted inside the trigger zone (red bubble)
   - Most historical storms below/left of threshold

--- page 20 ---
Historical trigger analysis table. "Analyses historiques du déclencheur" — full table of tropical systems affecting Haiti since 2000 with simulated trigger outcomes (EM-DAT population affected data):
Columns: Système, Mobilisation (True/False), Action Anticipatoire (True/False), Observationnel (True/False), Population affectée (EM-DAT)
Key entries (True triggers):
- Matthew 2016: True / True / True / 316,839
- Isaac 2012: True / True / True / ~90,000+
- Tomas 2010: True / True / True / ~3,000
- Ivan 2004: True / True / False / ~5,000
Systems that would not trigger (False/False for forecast triggers) but observed impact: Jeanne 2004 (~170,184), Sandy 2012, Ike 2008 (~129,508), Noël 2007 (~90,000), Gustav 2008 (~50,000), Hanna 2008 (~50,000), Laura 2020 (~38,000), Grace 2021 (~46,870).
Systems with no/very low impact: LB 2022 (~200), Fay 2008 (~190), Elsa 2021 (~8).

--- page 21 ---
Scatter plot: full historical precipitation vs wind comparison for threshold determination.
"Comparaison de précipitations, vent, et impact — Seuil de distance = 230 km":
- X-axis: Vitesse du vent maximum (noeuds), 0–140
- Y-axis: Précipitations produites (mm, 2-jour avg over all Haiti), 0–160
- Red upper-right zone: "Zone de déclenchement CERF en rouge"
- Matthew 2016: large red bubble inside trigger zone (~65 knots wind, ~155mm precip, largest population impact)
- Isaac 2012: in trigger zone (~60 knots, ~45mm)
- Tomas 2010: near threshold
- Most other storms (Jeanne, Sandy, Ike, etc.) below precipitation threshold despite significant impact
Section 3: Methodology for threshold setting in three stages. Explains process to determine optimal thresholds balancing sensitivity and specificity across historical record.

--- page 22 ---
Threshold methodology (final page). Simulation of all Atlantic tropical cyclones since 2000 using EM-DAT data and CHIRPS-GEFS/IMERG. Parameter space explored:
- D (distance from Haiti center km): 10, 20, 30, … 500
- N (wind speed noeuds): 5, 10, 15, … 180
- N (number of days to aggregate precipitation): 1, 2, 3 and duration system is close
- P (percentage of Haiti covered by precipitation): 10, 20, 30, … 90
- P (precipitation threshold mm): 10, 20, 30, … 170
Trigger fires if: system center ≤ D km from Haiti at any point; winds ≥ N noeuds; precipitation ≥ P mm over N days (at least X% of Haiti).
Final chosen threshold: distance ≤ 230 km, wind ≥ 64 noeuds (Cat 1), precipitation ≥ 42 mm for forecast triggers; 50 noeuds + 70 mm for observational trigger. Precipitation threshold intentionally set higher for observation (IMERG) vs forecast (CHIRPS-GEFS) due to systematic bias. Lead-time limit set at 48h for forecast trigger to avoid excessive false positives from long-range uncertainty. [End of document]
